title: Developmental cell programs are co-opted in inflammatory skin disease
quotes:
ILC1/NK have
overlapping features of ILC1 and NKs, as described

We identified 4 clusters of innate lymphocytes that were CD161(KLRB1)+
CD3(CD3D/CD3G)-, consisting of ILC1/3, ILC2, ILC1/Natural Killer (NK) and NK
(KLRD1+, GNLY+, PRF1+, GZMB+ and FCGR3A+) cells
summary: Reynolds et al. (2021) identify ILC1/NK as a distinct innate lymphocyte cluster with overlapping features of ILC1 and NK cells. This hybrid population is CD161(KLRB1)+CD3- and shares some NK cytotoxic markers while also expressing ILC1-associated features.

title: Natural killer cells in skin: a unique opportunity to better characterize the many facets of an overlooked secondary lymphoid organ
quotes: scRNA-seq of lesional vs non-lesional skin identified four unique clusters of innate lymphocytes in skin that were CD161(KLRB1)+/CD3(CD3D/CD3G)neg. These subgroups included ILC1/3, ILC2, ILC1/NK cells, and NK (KLRD1+, GNLY+, PRF1+, GZMB+, and FCGR3A+) cells
Healthy human dermis contains NK cells that are CD56+ and uniformly CD16a neg CCR7 neg, in contrast to circulating NK cells
summary: Johnson and Lee (2025) confirm the ILC1/NK hybrid cluster in adult skin. Tissue-resident NK cells in dermis are CD56+CD16a-CCR7-, distinct from circulating NK cells, reflecting the tissue-adaptation shared with ILC1.
